document.title: 3M MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET 3M(tm) Scotch-Grip (tm) Fastener Adhesive 2353 (Neutral)
standards: U.S. OSHA Hazard Communication Standard, 29 CFR 1910.1200

sourceSummary.oneLine: 3M MSDS for 3M(tm) Scotch-Grip (tm) Fastener Adhesive 2353 (Neutral), issued 10/14/2004.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Safety, handling, storage, exposure control, disposal, transport, and regulatory reference information for the adhesive product.
keyPoints: Product use is listed as assembly of motored machinery.
The product is a flammable liquid and vapor with OSHA Flammability Classification: Class IB Flammable Liquid.
The ingredient table lists toluene at 38% by weight.
The MSDS states it was prepared to meet the U.S. OSHA Hazard Communication Standard, 29 CFR 1910.1200.
California Proposition 65 information identifies toluene as a developmental toxin.

evidence.claim: The document is a Material Safety Data Sheet for 3M(tm) Scotch-Grip (tm) Fastener Adhesive 2353 (Neutral).
evidence.evidenceText: 3M MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET 3M(tm) Scotch-Grip (tm) Fastener Adhesive 2353 (Neutral) 10/14/2004

evidence.claim: The MSDS issue date is 10/14/2004.
evidence.evidenceText: Issue Date: 10/14/2004

evidence.claim: The document group is 19-0739-3 and it supersedes 03/16/2004.
evidence.evidenceText: Supercedes Date: 03/16/2004 Document Group: 19-0739-3

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is 3M and the division is 3M Canada Company.
evidence.evidenceText: MANUFACTURER: 3M DIVISION: 3M Canada Company

evidence.claim: The product specific use is assembly of motored machinery.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Use: Specific Use: Assembly of motored machinery

evidence.claim: The ingredient table lists toluene CAS 108-88-3 at 38% by weight.
evidence.evidenceText: TOLUENE 108-88-3 38

evidence.claim: Applied coated and dried versions of the adhesive contain the same ingredients except that toluene is absent.
evidence.evidenceText: Applied (i.e. coated and dried) versions of this adhesive contain the same ingredients except that toluene is absent.

evidence.claim: The product is described as a liquid with toluene-like odor and off-white to cream color.
evidence.evidenceText: Odor, Color, Grade: Toluene-like odor, off-white to cream General Physical Form: Liquid

evidence.claim: The emergency overview identifies flammable liquid and vapor hazards.
evidence.evidenceText: Flammable liquid and vapor.

evidence.claim: The product may cause chemical eye burns, severe skin irritation, allergic skin reaction, target organ effects, and reproductive harm.
evidence.evidenceText: May cause chemical eye burns. May cause severe skin irritation. May cause allergic skin reaction. May cause target organ effects. Contains a chemical or chemicals which can cause birth defects or other reproductive harm.

evidence.claim: Eye contact first aid requires flushing eyes with water for at least 15 minutes and immediate medical attention.
evidence.evidenceText: Eye Contact: Immediately flush eyes with large amounts of water for at least 15 minutes. Get immediate medical attention.

evidence.claim: Flash point is approximately 4 ºC by Tagliabue Closed Cup.
evidence.evidenceText: Flash Point Approximately 4 ºC [Test Method: Tagliabue Closed Cup] [Details: 40 deg F]

evidence.claim: OSHA Flammability Classification is Class IB Flammable Liquid.
evidence.evidenceText: OSHA Flammability Classification: Class IB Flammable Liquid

evidence.claim: Recommended extinguishing media are Class B extinguishing agents such as dry chemical or carbon dioxide.
evidence.evidenceText: Use fire extinguishers with class B extinguishing agents (e.g., dry chemical, carbon dioxide).

evidence.claim: Handling instructions include industrial or professional use only and keeping away from ignition sources.
evidence.evidenceText: For industrial or professional use only. Keep away from heat, sparks, open flame, pilot lights and other sources of ignition.

evidence.claim: Eye protection recommendation is indirect vented goggles.
evidence.evidenceText: The following eye protection(s) are recommended: Indirect Vented Goggles.

evidence.claim: Respiratory protection selection references NIOSH approved respirators and OSHA regulations.
evidence.evidenceText: Select one of the following NIOSH approved respirators based on airborne concentration of contaminants and in accordance with OSHA regulations: Half facepiece or fullface air-purifying respirator with organic vapor cartridges.

evidence.claim: The exposure guidelines list toluene ACGIH TWA at 50 ppm with skin notation.
evidence.evidenceText: TOLUENE ACGIH TWA 50 ppm Skin Notation*; Table A4

evidence.claim: The exposure guidelines list toluene OSHA TWA at 200 ppm under Table Z-2.
evidence.evidenceText: TOLUENE OSHA TWA 200 ppm Table Z-2

evidence.claim: Waste disposal guidance identifies EPA RCRA hazardous waste number D001 ignitable.
evidence.evidenceText: EPA Hazardous Waste Number (RCRA): D001 (Ignitable)
evidence.pageTarget: RCRA Hazardous Waste Number D001 Ignitable

evidence.claim: The ID number listed for transport information is 70-0710-6191-8.
evidence.pageTarget: 3M Scotch-Grip Fastener Adhesive 2353 Neutral Transport Information

evidence.claim: The 311/312 hazard categories mark fire hazard, immediate hazard, and delayed hazard as yes.
evidence.evidenceText: 311/312 Hazard Categories: Fire Hazard - Yes Pressure Hazard - No Reactivity Hazard - No Immediate Hazard - Yes Delayed Hazard - Yes

evidence.claim: EPCRA Section 313 toxic chemical reporting identifies toluene CAS 108-88-3 at 38% by weight.
evidence.evidenceText: Section 313 Toxic Chemicals subject to the reporting requirements of that section and 40 CFR part 372 (EPCRA): Ingredient C.A.S. No % by Wt TOLUENE 108-88-3 38

evidence.claim: California Proposition 65 identifies toluene CAS 108-88-3 as a developmental toxin.
evidence.evidenceText: CALIFORNIA PROPOSITION 65 Ingredient C.A.S. No. Classification TOLUENE 108-88-3 *Developmental Toxin

evidence.claim: The product components are listed on the Canadian Domestic Substances List.
evidence.evidenceText: The components of this product are listed on the Canadian Domestic Substances List.

evidence.claim: The product components comply with TSCA chemical notification requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: The components of this product are in compliance with the chemical notification requirements of TSCA.

evidence.claim: The MSDS was prepared to meet the U.S. OSHA Hazard Communication Standard, 29 CFR 1910.1200.
evidence.evidenceText: This MSDS has been prepared to meet the U.S. OSHA Hazard Communication Standard, 29 CFR 1910.1200.
evidence.pageTarget: U.S. OSHA Hazard Communication Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200

evidence.claim: NFPA hazard classification is Health 2, Flammability 3, Reactivity 0, Special Hazards None.
evidence.evidenceText: NFPA Hazard Classification Health: 2 Flammability: 3 Reactivity: 0 Special Hazards: None

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: the document is issued by 3M and references regulatory frameworks, but it is not itself an authority-issued approval or certificate.
No formal revision number is provided; only Document Group 19-0739-3, Issue Date 10/14/2004, and Supercedes Date 03/16/2004 are present.
Source metadata productSignal is CYAN, but the source text identifies a 3M adhesive MSDS and does not mention CYAN.
